_meta.purpose: Wave-1 catalog of consumer class action settlements relevant to the credit.com / Lexington Law population, for the credit.com recovery report. Organized by friction: 'frictionless' = already settled, class member gets paid with no/minimal action; 'claim_filing' = a claim must be submitted (workflow we run); 'mass_arb' = rolling individual-arbitration sign-up; 'structural' = recurring defendants where new claims can be originated.

tiers.1: field-provable: our data fields directly satisfy the class-definition elements (creditor + account type + date window)
tiers.2: proxy + attestation: data establishes the plausible pool (relationship with defendant in period); the gating fact (received call/text/notice, event) must be attested by the claimant or confirmed by the administrator

_meta.cohort_counts_source: casepeople sharded index (rederive v2, LEX + CCOM for capital one; others LEX until CCOM reprocess)
_meta.status: wave 1 — sweep of Top Class Actions, ClassAction.org, openclassactions, settlement sites + administrator pages, 2026-06-10. Deeper per-settlement verification and cr_db sizing queries pending.

frictionless.settlement: In re Capital One 360 Savings Account Interest Rate Litigation
frictionless.status: Final approval; automatic payments ~2026-07-21 by check/electronic
frictionless.classDefinition: Holders of Capital One 360 Savings accounts during the period Capital One offered the higher-rate 360 Performance Savings without converting/notifying existing 360 Savings customers (approx. Sept 2019 onward)
frictionless.perClaimant: Pro rata on lost interest; no claim form needed
frictionless.actionNeeded: None if address/payment info current; monitor for check. Exclusion already closed.

dataMapping.note: Deposit accounts do not appear on credit reports. Proxy = any Capital One relationship in file + outreach asking about 360 Savings. Cohort ceiling (any Capital One tradeline): 1,712,966.

frictionless.status: OPEN — current accountholders paid automatically; former accountholders must file by 2026-06-29
frictionless.classDefinition: BofA accountholders charged more than one out-of-network balance inquiry fee in the same visit to an FCTI ATM at a 7-Eleven, 2018-05-01 to 2021-11-16, who did not get paid in Weiss v. FCTI
frictionless.actionNeeded: None for current accountholders; claim form for former ones

dataMapping.note: BofA relationship provable (cohort 333,611); the ATM event is not in credit data — attestation. Split current vs former accountholder matters.

frictionless.status: PAYING — administrator began mailing checks 2026-02-13; automatic, no claim
frictionless.classDefinition: Federal student loan borrowers harmed by Navient servicing failures (forbearance steering etc.) per CFPB order
frictionless.actionNeeded: None; ensure current address with administrator (Epiq)

dataMapping.note: Navient student-loan tradeline directly identifies servicer relationship. Cohort: 451,340.

frictionless.status: AUTOMATIC — CA class paid without claim form (optional digital-pay election)

dataMapping.note: MCM tradeline/collection + CA address. Cohort (national): 241,303; filter state=CA.

frictionless.classDefinition: VA consumers per settlement (see case-intake workbook)

claim_filing.defendant: PHH Mortgage / PHH Corp. and affiliates
claim_filing.settlement: Munoz v. PHH Corp. — captive mortgage reinsurance kickbacks (RESPA)
claim_filing.fund: fixed per-loan payments, no pro-rata reduction
claim_filing.proofRequired: Claim form; class list is loan-based
claim_filing.classDefinition: Persons who obtained residential mortgage loans originated and/or acquired by PHH or affiliates 2007-01-01 to 2009-12-31, purchased private mortgage insurance, and whose loans were in PHH's captive mortgage reinsurance agreements (incl. successors/heirs/assigns)

dataMapping.note: Mortgage tradeline with creditor ~ PHH AND open_date 2007-2009. PMI/captive-reinsurance element not visible in data but loan vintage + creditor is a strong screen; administrator list confirms. MEASURED 2026-06-10: PHH any-vintage = 29,679 LEX + 14,552 CCOM people; 2007-2009 vintage = 4,180 LEX + 1,624 CCOM (~5,800 combined, overlap not deduped) — at $875 fixed/loan that is a ~$5.1M claim ceiling before the captive-reinsurance screen. Ocwen (acquirer, context only): 52,681 LEX. Spellings: PHH MORTGAGE SERVICES / PHH MORT SVC / PHH MORTGAGE ICE CENTER.

claim_filing.perClaimant: ~$60 cash (up to $599) no-proof tier; up to $25,000 documented losses; +$100 CA CCPA; 3yr credit monitoring
claim_filing.proofRequired: Claim ID from mailed/emailed notice required to file; documented-loss tier needs receipts
claim_filing.classDefinition: ~2,187,170 U.S. individuals whose personal information was exposed in Flagstar's two 2021 cyberattacks

dataMapping.note: Flagstar mortgage/banking tradeline in 2020-2021 = plausible class member; gate is the breach notice (Claim ID). Workflow: locate notice or administrator lookup by name/address. MEASURED 2026-06-10: 24,158 LEX + 14,260 CCOM people with Flagstar tradelines (any window; spellings FLAGSTAR BANK / FLAGSTAR BK / NYCB AKA FLAGSTAR BANK). Class is 2.19M nationally, so expect a meaningful subset to hold Claim IDs.

claim_filing.settlement: Citrix Bleed data breach (Oct 16-19, 2023) class settlement — Kroll administering
claim_filing.perClaimant: $50 cash base; up to $10,000 documented losses
claim_filing.proofRequired: No proof for $50 base tier; documentation for loss tier
claim_filing.classDefinition: ~31.6M U.S. individuals whose personal information was compromised in the Comcast/Xfinity breach of Oct 16-19, 2023

dataMapping.note: Xfinity is a utility/telecom relationship — usually NOT a tradeline. MEASURED 2026-06-10: only 4,492 LEX + 1,029 CCOM people show Comcast/Xfinity tradelines or collections. The real play is population overlap: ~31.6M national class against a 10M contactable base means roughly 1 in 10 of the base is plausibly a class member by national incidence — outreach/attestation channel, not creditor matching.

claim_filing.proofRequired: Login ID + PIN from notice (administrator can resend)
claim_filing.classDefinition: U.S. individuals whose financial account/routing numbers were exposed in the 2024-08-17..19 breach or who received notice (~77,000 people)

dataMapping.note: Small class (77K). Fidelity not typically a tradeline. Low expected overlap — include for completeness only.

claim_filing.proofRequired: Claim form; attestation (verify whether phone-number matching against dialer records)
claim_filing.classDefinition: U.S. residents who received an artificial/prerecorded voice call on a cell phone from Hy Cite 2020-10-22 to 2025-09-10 and were NOT Hy Cite customers

dataMapping.note: Pure attestation — credit data cannot show call receipt; phone numbers on file enable administrator dialer-list matching. High per-claimant value makes outreach worthwhile.

claim_filing.proofRequired: Proof of cell number that received the call
claim_filing.classDefinition: Persons called by Register.com with artificial/prerecorded voice 2021-02-12 to 2025-11-24 after the number was permanently disconnected and listed in the FCC Reassigned Numbers Database

dataMapping.note: Very narrow (reassigned numbers). Phone numbers on file could be cross-checked, but 5-day window — likely skip unless trivial.

claim_filing.settlement: Jackson v. Athena Bitcoin — TCPA/FTSA texts — $4.5M
claim_filing.classDefinition: See case-intake workbook Claim Requirements sheet

claim_filing.settlement: Ford v. Genesis — Maryland unlicensed lending (interest/fees)
claim_filing.classDefinition: Maryland consumers with Genesis-serviced accounts (see workbook)

dataMapping.note: Genesis/Milestone tradeline + MD address. Cohort token: milestone genesis (indexed).

claim_filing.proofRequired: Yes — vehicle ownership/lease, software-upgrade status, theft/attempt documentation (police report/insurance)
claim_filing.classDefinition: Owners/lessees of 2011-2022 Hyundai/Kia vehicles without factory engine immobilizer, software upgrade installed or scheduled, qualifying theft/attempt on/after 2025-04-29 and before zinc-sleeve install or 2027-03-31

dataMapping.note: Auto tradelines with Hyundai Capital/Kia Finance identify owners; theft event requires claimant docs. Long window = good pipeline case. MEASURED 2026-06-10: Hyundai finance = 44,385 LEX + 15,530 CCOM; Kia finance = 54,466 LEX + 15,386 CCOM (overlap between brands/populations not deduped). Model year 2011-2022 + turn-key ignition narrows further; financing dates ~2011-2022 are the proxy.

claim_filing.defendant: Lakeview Loan Servicing / Bayview Asset Management / Pingora / Community Loan Servicing
claim_filing.settlement: In re Lakeview Loan Servicing Data Breach Litigation (Oct 11, 2021 breach)
claim_filing.perClaimant: Pro rata cash + up to $5,000 documented out-of-pocket; extra pro rata for CA residents
claim_filing.proofRequired: Claim form; documented-loss tier needs receipts; class = breach-notice recipients
claim_filing.classDefinition: U.S. residents who received a notice from Bayview Asset Management, Lakeview Loan Servicing, Pingora Loan Servicing, or Community Loan Servicing that the Oct 11, 2021 breach potentially compromised their PII

dataMapping.note: MEASURED 2026-06-10: essentially absent from tradelines (~195 LEX matches, mostly UNRELATED 'Lakeview' credit unions/financial cos — name collision). Lakeview subservices via LoanCare, so it rarely appears as the reporting creditor. Outreach/attestation only; deprioritize given 6/22 deadline.

claim_filing.perClaimant: $25-$10,400 depending on tier; crypto-theft claims from separate pool
claim_filing.proofRequired: Notice-gated (email notice from LastPass Nov-Dec 2022 or settlement notice); crypto claims need documentation
claim_filing.classDefinition: U.S. persons/entities whose LastPass account data was exposed in the Aug-Nov 2022 incident and who had data stored at the time, per LastPass notice

dataMapping.note: No credit-data signal; outreach-only candidate for the email base.

claim_filing.settlement: FTC dark-patterns enrollment case — consumer redress
claim_filing.classDefinition: Consumers enrolled in Prime via challenged flows (FTC will define; many paid automatically, others claim)

dataMapping.note: Universal-population play for the email base; no credit-data gate.
Exeter auto tradeline directly identifies the relationship. Cohort: 235,993.

structural_recurring.basis: TCPA autodialer collection calls — repeat defendant
Subprime auto — multistate AG settlements, repossession practices
FDCPA/repossession — Klare settlement closed 2026-05-18 but recurring
FDCPA — perennial defendant; Leedeman CA automatic
Servicer misconduct — CFPB redress paying now; state cases recurring
